problem_statement:
Given the head of a singly linked list. Group all the nodes with odd indices followed by all the nodes with even indices and return the reordered list.



Consider the 1st node to have index 1 and so on. The relative order of the elements inside the odd and even group must remain the same as the given input.

examples.explanation:
Odd Nodes in LinkedList are 1,3,5 and Even Nodes in LinkedList are 2,4,6
In Modified LinkedList all even Nodes comes before all Odd Nodes. So Modified LinkedList looks like 2창혛혪4창혛혪6창혛혪1창혛혪3창혛혪5창혛혪Null. Order of even and odd Nodes is 
maintained in modified LinkedList.
As there are no Even Nodes in LinkedList, The Modified LinkedList is same as Original LinkedList.
